message.content: i previously had some work on a icon library for bim-ai, but my computer crashed. where are we in that project?

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.description: Search session history for icon library work
input.prompt: Search for any previous work on an icon library or icons package in the bim-ai project. Look for mentions of "icons", "icon library", "packages/icons", "IconGallery", or related terms. Summarize what was done and where the work left off.

content.text:
Based on my search through the project history, here's what I found about the icon library work:

## Summary of Icon Library Work in bim-ai

### What Was Done

The project has a comprehensive **@bim-ai/icons** package created recently (May 6, 2026). This is a BIM-native SVG icon library with the following structure:

**Completed Components:**
- **Base icon infrastructure** (`/packages/icons/`)
  - `icon.tsx` — Base `BimIcon` component type compatible with lucide-react API
  - 12 category files with organized icon definitions:
    - `tools.tsx` — Drawing tools (Wall, Door, Window, Floor, Roof, Stair, Railing, Room, Dimension, Section, Tag, CurtainWall, Column, Beam)
    - `views.tsx` — View types and annotations (Plan, Section, Elevation, 3D, Sheet, Schedule, etc.)
    - `organization.tsx` — Family, Assembly, Material, Phase, Issue, Clash icons
    - `mep.tsx` — MEP systems (Duct, Pipe, Cable, Conduit, Equipment, Fixtures)
    - `structural.tsx` — Structural elements (Foundation, Truss, Brace, Rebar)
    - `architectural.tsx` — Architectural features (Ceiling, Ramp, Curtain Panel, Skylight)
    - `site.tsx` — Site/civil elements (Topography, Property line, Parking, Planting, Road)
    - `annotation.tsx` — Annotation symbols (Revision Cloud, Break Line, Keynote, Match Line, Scale Bar)
    - `workflow.tsx` — Data/coordination (Workset, Design Option, Revision, RFI, Point Cloud, IFC, LOD)
    - `edit.tsx` — Geometry operations (Mirror, Array, Align, Split, Trim, Void, Pin)
  - `index.ts` — Central export registry with 100+ icon exports organized by category/phase
  - `package.json` — Properly configured private package with React peer dependencies
  - `tsconfig.json` — TypeScript configuration

**UI Integration:**
- **IconGallery component** (`packages/web/src/design-systems/IconGallery.tsx`)
  - Interactive showcase of all 100+ icons
  - Searchable interface with filter-by-name and filter-by-export functionality
  - Size and stroke-width controls (sizes: 16, 18, 20, 24, 32; strokes: 1, 1.5, 2)
  - Click-to-copy import statements feature
  - Organized into 9 logical sections matching the icon categories

### Where Work Left Off

The icon library was committed in session `9ccda089-cee5-43cf-9e07-be3ed2780126` on May 6, 2026 at 11:35:52. The files are currently **untracked** in the working directory—they exist locally but haven't been committed to the main branch yet.

**Next steps would be:**
1. **Commit the icon package** to version control
2. **Add the icons package to the UI package exports** if needed for wider consumption
3. **Integrate IconGallery into the design system documentation** (currently it's a standalone component)
4. **Continue adding category expansions** (each category has a "Phase N" pattern suggesting there may be more phases planned)
5. **Test icon rendering across different viewports** and contexts

**File locations:**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/` — Icon library package
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/design-systems/IconGallery.tsx` — Interactive showcase
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/ui/src/icons.tsx` — Original chrome icon registry (from earlier feature)

content.text:
Based on my search through the project history, here's what I found about the icon library work:

## Summary of Icon Library Work in bim-ai

### What Was Done

The project has a comprehensive **@bim-ai/icons** package created recently (May 6, 2026). This is a BIM-native SVG icon library with the following structure:

**Completed Components:**
- **Base icon infrastructure** (`/packages/icons/`)
  - `icon.tsx` — Base `BimIcon` component type compatible with lucide-react API
  - 12 category files with organized icon definitions:
    - `tools.tsx` — Drawing tools (Wall, Door, Window, Floor, Roof, Stair, Railing, Room, Dimension, Section, Tag, CurtainWall, Column, Beam)
    - `views.tsx` — View types and annotations (Plan, Section, Elevation, 3D, Sheet, Schedule, etc.)
    - `organization.tsx` — Family, Assembly, Material, Phase, Issue, Clash icons
    - `mep.tsx` — MEP systems (Duct, Pipe, Cable, Conduit, Equipment, Fixtures)
    - `structural.tsx` — Structural elements (Foundation, Truss, Brace, Rebar)
    - `architectural.tsx` — Architectural features (Ceiling, Ramp, Curtain Panel, Skylight)
    - `site.tsx` — Site/civil elements (Topography, Property line, Parking, Planting, Road)
    - `annotation.tsx` — Annotation symbols (Revision Cloud, Break Line, Keynote, Match Line, Scale Bar)
    - `workflow.tsx` — Data/coordination (Workset, Design Option, Revision, RFI, Point Cloud, IFC, LOD)
    - `edit.tsx` — Geometry operations (Mirror, Array, Align, Split, Trim, Void, Pin)
  - `index.ts` — Central export registry with 100+ icon exports organized by category/phase
  - `package.json` — Properly configured private package with React peer dependencies
  - `tsconfig.json` — TypeScript configuration

**UI Integration:**
- **IconGallery component** (`packages/web/src/design-systems/IconGallery.tsx`)
  - Interactive showcase of all 100+ icons
  - Searchable interface with filter-by-name and filter-by-export functionality
  - Size and stroke-width controls (sizes: 16, 18, 20, 24, 32; strokes: 1, 1.5, 2)
  - Click-to-copy import statements feature
  - Organized into 9 logical sections matching the icon categories

### Where Work Left Off

The icon library was committed in session `9ccda089-cee5-43cf-9e07-be3ed2780126` on May 6, 2026 at 11:35:52. The files are currently **untracked** in the working directory—they exist locally but haven't been committed to the main branch yet.

**Next steps would be:**
1. **Commit the icon package** to version control
2. **Add the icons package to the UI package exports** if needed for wider consumption
3. **Integrate IconGallery into the design system documentation** (currently it's a standalone component)
4. **Continue adding category expansions** (each category has a "Phase N" pattern suggesting there may be more phases planned)
5. **Test icon rendering across different viewports** and contexts

**File locations:**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/icons/` — Icon library package
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/design-systems/IconGallery.tsx` — Interactive showcase
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/ui/src/icons.tsx` — Original chrome icon registry (from earlier feature)

Here's where things stand:

**The work is intact** — it's all in your local repo, just uncommitted. The `packages/icons/` directory is untracked (never committed to git), which is why it survived outside of any branch.

### What was built

A full `@bim-ai/icons` package with 100+ BIM-native SVG icons across 10 categories:
- `tools.tsx` — Wall, Door, Window, Floor, Roof, Stair, Railing, etc.
- `views.tsx` — Plan, Section, Elevation, 3D, Sheet, Schedule, etc.
- `mep.tsx`, `structural.tsx`, `architectural.tsx`, `site.tsx`
- `annotation.tsx`, `workflow.tsx`, `edit.tsx`, `organization.tsx`

Plus an interactive `IconGallery.tsx` component in `packages/web/src/design-systems/` — searchable, filterable, with size/stroke controls and click-to-copy import statements.

### Current status

- **`packages/icons/`** — fully written, never committed (untracked)
- **`packages/web/src/design-systems/IconGallery.tsx`** — also untracked
- **`packages/ui/src/icons.tsx`** — modified (staged or unstaged), appears to be an integration point

The logical next step would be to commit the icons package and wire up the `IconGallery` into the app's design system docs/route. Want me to do that, or pick up somewhere specific?

1	/**
2	 * @bim-ai/icons — BIM-native SVG icon library.
3	 *
4	 * Drop-in compatible with lucide-react's icon API:
5	 *
6	 *   import { WallIcon, DoorIcon } from '@bim-ai/icons'
7	 *   <WallIcon size={18} strokeWidth={1.5} className="text-stone-600" />
8	 *
9	 * All icons are 24×24 viewBox, stroke-based, currentColor.
10	 * Props: size, strokeWidth, absoluteStrokeWidth, plus any SVG attribute.
11	 */
12

14

file.content:
/**
 * @bim-ai/icons — BIM-native SVG icon library.
 *
 * Drop-in compatible with lucide-react's icon API:
 *
 *   import { WallIcon, DoorIcon } from '@bim-ai/icons'
 *   <WallIcon size={18} strokeWidth={1.5} className="text-stone-600" />
 *
 * All icons are 24×24 viewBox, stroke-based, currentColor.
 * Props: size, strokeWidth, absoluteStrokeWidth, plus any SVG attribute.
 */

export type { BimIcon, BimIconProps } from './icon';

// Phase 1 — drawing tools
export {
  WallIcon,
  DoorIcon,
  WindowIcon,
  FloorIcon,
  RoofIcon,
  StairIcon,
  RailingIcon,
  RoomIcon,
  DimensionIcon,
  SectionIcon,
  TagIcon,
  CurtainWallIcon,
  ColumnIcon,
  BeamIcon,
} from './tools';

// Phase 2 — views & annotations (original)
export {
  PlanViewIcon,
  SectionViewIcon,
  ElevationViewIcon,
  OrbitViewIcon,
  SheetIcon,
  ScheduleViewIcon,
  CalloutIcon,
  ViewpointIcon,
  SectionBoxIcon,
  GridLineIcon,
  LevelIcon,
  DetailLineIcon,
} from './views';

// Phase 3 — organization & coordination (original)
export {
  FamilyIcon,
  FamilyTypeIcon,
  GroupIcon,
  AssemblyIcon,
  LinkedModelIcon,
  MaterialIcon,
  WallLayerIcon,
  PhaseIcon,
  IssueIcon,
  ClashIcon,
  ValidationRuleIcon,
  DeviationIcon,
} from './organization';

// MEP — mechanical, electrical & plumbing
export {
  DuctRectIcon,
  DuctRoundIcon,
  PipeIcon,
  CableTrayIcon,
  ConduitIcon,
  MechanicalEquipmentIcon,
  PlumbingFixtureIcon,
  LightingFixtureIcon,
  ElectricalPanelIcon,
  FireSprinklerIcon,
  DiffuserIcon,
  MepSpaceIcon,
} from './mep';

// Structural
export {
  FoundationIcon,
  StripFootingIcon,
  TrussIcon,
  BraceIcon,
  RebarIcon,
  StructuralConnectionIcon,
} from './structural';

// Architectural additions
export {
  CeilingIcon,
  OpeningIcon,
  ShaftOpeningIcon,
  RampIcon,
  MassIcon,
  CurtainPanelIcon,
  MullionIcon,
  SkyLightIcon,
  PartitionIcon,
} from './architectural';

// Site & civil
export {
  TopoIcon,
  PropertyLineIcon,
  ParkingSpaceIcon,
  PlantingIcon,
  RoadIcon,
  RetainingWallIcon,
  NorthArrowIcon,
} from './site';

// Annotation & documentation
export {
  RevisionCloudIcon,
  BreakLineIcon,
  CentreLineIcon,
  KeynoteIcon,
  MatchLineIcon,
  ScaleBarIcon,
  AreaLabelIcon,
  SpotElevationIcon,
  SlopeArrowIcon,
} from './annotation';

// Workflow, coordination & data
export {
  WorksetIcon,
  DesignOptionIcon,
  RevisionIcon,
  RFIIcon,
  SubmittalIcon,
  PointCloudIcon,
  DigitalTwinIcon,
  SyncIcon,
  TransmittalIcon,
  IFCIcon,
  LODIcon,
  QuantityTakeoffIcon,
  EnergyModelIcon,
  ScopeBoxIcon,
} from './workflow';

// Edit / geometry operations
export {
  MirrorIcon,
  ArrayLinearIcon,
  ArrayRadialIcon,
  AlignIcon,
  SplitIcon,
  TrimExtendIcon,
  VoidIcon,
  PinIcon,
  UnpinIcon,
} from './edit';

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(icons): add @bim-ai/icons package with 100+ BIM-native SVG icons

Introduces a new `packages/icons` workspace package containing stroke-based,
lucide-compatible icons across 10 BIM categories (tools, views, MEP,
structural, architectural, site, annotation, workflow, edit, organization).

Wires the package into @bim-ai/ui re-exports and adds an interactive
IconGallery at /icons (searchable, size/stroke controls, click-to-copy
import statements).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Committed as `3489e8e1`. Everything is now in version control:

- `packages/icons/` — the full `@bim-ai/icons` package (100+ icons across 10 categories)
- `packages/web/src/design-systems/IconGallery.tsx` — the interactive gallery
- Route live at `/icons`, icons re-exported through `@bim-ai/ui`

The remaining modified files (ToolPalette, Inspector, Cheatsheet, etc.) are other in-progress features and are still unstaged.
